summary: McKenzie shares his delight at discovering that a fellow Joel on Software forum regular makes niche software for chimney sweeps. He finds the ultra-specific niche charming and references the Mary Poppins chimney sweep song to underscore the whimsy of the product.
key_points: Celebrates the existence of extremely niche micro-ISV software targeting chimney sweeps
Highlights the charm and viability of serving overlooked, tiny markets with specialized software
References the Joel on Software community as a hub for independent software developers
money_quotes: If the prospect of this doesn't make you smile, you have neither soul nor taste in Disney movies.
key_insight: There is real business opportunity and delight in building software for the most unexpected, hyper-specific niches.
